goal: Implement a regime-invariant neural intensity model of stock-movement ONSET (启动子), with PU/weak-supervised labels and a leakage-safe, cluster-robust evaluation protocol. This operationalizes the frontier directions recommended for the onset concept. The contribution is the FORMALIZATION + reference method + benchmark, NOT raw predictive alpha (the signal is known to be weak).
acceptanceCriteria: verifyCommand (pytest tests/algo) passes
Every task ships a hermetic test (synthetic data, <5s, no network, no GPU required) that fails before the deliverable exists and passes after
The GATE is 'component runs and produces structurally-valid output', NOT 'beats a baseline' — weak signal must never block completion
No look-ahead: any feature/label builder is point-in-time safe (asserted in tests)

tasks.title: PU-learning onset label builder
tasks.rationale: The onset label is ill-defined; 'non-onset' is Unlabeled, not Negative. Frames labeling as Positive-Unlabeled learning (nnPU risk).
acceptanceCriteria: build_pu_sets(df, positive_mask) returns disjoint Positive / Unlabeled index sets
nnpu_risk(scores, labels, prior_pi) returns a finite scalar and respects the non-negative correction
prior pi is a configurable argument (default from positive rate)
test uses synthetic data only and asserts shapes + that risk decreases when scores align with true positives
tasks.notes: DONE: src/onset/pu_labels.py (build_pu_sets/class_prior/nnpu_risk, Kiryo 2017 nnPU, sigmoid surrogate); 5 hermetic tests green.

tasks.title: Weak-supervision label model over expert rules
tasks.rationale: Turn V12.31 expert rules (bottoms_rising / above_5d_low_5pct / ma_pattern_ok / volume_boost) from hard rules into labeling functions aggregated by a generative label model (Snorkel-style). Directly upgrades contribution C1.
acceptanceCriteria: label_model(lf_matrix) accepts an N x K matrix of LF votes in {-1,0,+1} (0=abstain)
returns soft labels in [0,1] per anchor and an estimated accuracy per LF
majority-vote fallback is available; abstains are handled
synthetic test: when one LF is near-perfect and others noisy, the model upweights the good LF
tasks.notes: DONE: src/onset/weak_supervision.py (majority_vote + EM-like label_model, accuracy-weighted log-odds, soft labels in [0,1], abstain->0.5). 4 hermetic tests green.

tasks.title: Neural onset-intensity head (temporal point process)
tasks.rationale: Reframe onset as an event intensity lambda(t) on top of the existing TCN encoder, not a 5-day return class. Matches the '起点' semantics; enables self/cross-exciting (sector contagion) later.
acceptanceCriteria: OnsetIntensityHead consumes encoder features [B,T,D] and outputs positive intensity [B,T] via softplus
discrete_time_nll(intensity, event_mask) returns a finite loss
runs on CPU; no XPU/GPU required for the test
synthetic test: loss is finite and decreases over a few optimizer steps on a trivial fit
tasks.notes: DONE: src/models/onset_intensity.py (OnsetIntensityHead softplus lambda>=0; discrete_time_nll Bernoulli-hazard p=1-exp(-lambda)). Decoupled from encoder (consumes [B,T,D]). 3 CPU tests green.

tasks.title: Regime-invariant trainer (IRM / HRM)
tasks.rationale: Directly attacks contribution C4 (cross-quarter heterogeneity, oracle headroom uncaptured). Environments = walk-forward splits/quarters; learn an invariant onset mechanism. NOTE: IRM only helps under diverse spurious correlations — keep test structural.
acceptanceCriteria: irm_penalty(loss, logits) returns a finite gradient-penalty scalar
train_step aggregates ERM loss across environments + lambda * IRM penalty
synthetic 2-environment test with one spurious feature: total penalty is finite and decreases over steps
lambda (penalty weight) and environment list are configurable
tasks.notes: Keep assertions structural; do NOT assert it beats ERM (known to be regime-dependent).

tasks.title: Leakage-safe, cluster-robust onset evaluator
tasks.rationale: Unify C3 (date-clustered bootstrap) + C5 (leakage guard) into one evaluation entry for onset models.
acceptanceCriteria: clustered_bootstrap(metric_fn, preds, target, dates) returns mean + 95% CI by resampling whole dates
point_in_time_guard(feature_dates, target_date) raises if any feature timestamp >= target_date
synthetic test: clustered CI is wider than the anchor-independent CI; PIT guard raises on a leaked feature
tasks.notes: Reuse bootstrap logic from src/evaluation/c3_dose_response.py / cluster_bootstrap.

tasks.title: Cutoff-controlled leakage probe (high-ROI: makes C5 causal)
tasks.rationale: Run the no-context ablation split by pre/post a configurable model-training-cutoff date. If post-cutoff accuracy collapses to chance, temporal leakage is proven causally (not just 'implausibly high').
acceptanceCriteria: split_by_cutoff(df, cutoff_date) returns pre/post frames
leakage_flag(acc_pre, acc_post, chance=0.5, tol) returns True when pre is high but post ~ chance
synthetic test: fabricated pre(high)/post(chance) data triggers the flag; matched-high data does not
tasks.notes: Reuse src/eval_e3 plumbing; this is the experiment recommended to upgrade C5 from suggestive to causal.

tasks.title: Differentiable ranking / utility objective
tasks.rationale: Cross-entropy barely beats random (ln3); train on the deployment objective. Differentiable soft-rank IC + top-K / Sharpe-style utility.
acceptanceCriteria: soft_rank_ic_loss(scores, returns) is differentiable and finite
topk_utility_loss(scores, returns, k) is differentiable and finite
synthetic test: gradient flows (requires_grad), and perfectly-ranked scores give near-optimal loss

tasks.title: Integration: regime-invariant neural onset model end-to-end
tasks.rationale: Wire encoder + intensity head (T-003) + IRM trainer (T-004) + PU/weak labels (T-001/T-002) + ranking objective (T-007), evaluated by the cluster-robust evaluator (T-005).
acceptanceCriteria: train_onset runs 1 tiny epoch on synthetic walk-forward-shaped data end-to-end
produces a metrics dict via src/evaluation/onset_eval.py and writes results/onset/<run>/stats.json
smoke test runs on CPU in <30s and asserts the stats file is written with expected keys
tasks.notes: Depends on T-001..T-005, T-007. This is the reference method for the paper, not a production model.
